title: Extinction Risk Estimation
text: goal extr estimate finite-horizon extinction risk population time series density-independent (drifted Wiener) model, support naive MLE observation-error--autocovariance-robust (OEAR) variance estimation.

title: Extinction Risk Estimation
code: # CRAN release install.packages("extr")  # GitHub version (may be newer than CRAN) # install.packages("remotes") # if needed remotes::install_github("hakoyamah/extr")

title: Extinction Risk Estimation
text: ext_di() estimates population growth rate, variance, extinction probability time series population sizes. supports two variance estimators: default naive maximum-likelihood estimator (method = "naive") observation-error--autocovariance-robust estimator (method = "oear"). Confidence intervals extinction probability based ww-zz method.

title: Extinction Risk Estimation
text: following example uses Yellowstone grizzly bear time series Dennis et al. (1991), digitized Fig. 5. published series running 3-year sum (3-year moving total). Probability decline 1 individual within 100 years: Probability decline 10 individuals within 100 years: QQ plot:  Using OEAR variance estimator:
code: library(extr) dat <- data.frame(   Time = 1959:1987,   Population = c(     44, 47, 46, 44, 46, 45, 46, 40, 39, 39, 42, 44, 41, 40,     33, 36, 34, 39, 35, 34, 38, 36, 37, 41, 39, 51, 47, 57, 47   ) ) ext_di(dat, th = 100) #> --- Estimates --- #>                                                       Estimate #> Probability of decline to 1 within 100 years (MLE): 9.4128e-05 #> Growth rate (MLE):                                   0.0023556 #> Environmental variance (MLE):                          0.01087 #> Unbiased variance:                                    0.011273 #> AIC for the distribution of N:                          165.06 #>                                                                        CI #> Probability of decline to 1 within 100 years (MLE):  (1.4586e-13, 0.5653) #> Growth rate (MLE):                                  (-0.038814, 0.043525) #> Environmental variance (MLE):                       (0.0070464, 0.020885) #> Unbiased variance:                                                      - #> AIC for the distribution of N:                                          - #>  #> --- Data Summary --- #>                               Value #> Current population size, nq:     47 #> xd = ln(nq / ne):            3.8501 #> Sample size, q + 1:              29 #>  #> --- Input Parameters --- #>                                                         Parameter #> Time unit:                                                  years #> Extinction threshold of population size, ne:                    1 #> Time window for extinction risk evaluation (years), th:     100.0 #> Significance level, alpha:                                   0.05 ext_di(dat, th = 100, ne = 10) #> --- Estimates --- #>                                                       Estimate #> Probability of decline to 10 within 100 years (MLE):  0.096852 #> Growth rate (MLE):                                   0.0023556 #> Environmental variance (MLE):                          0.01087 #> Unbiased variance:                                    0.011273 #> AIC for the distribution of N:                          165.06 #>                                                                         CI #> Probability of decline to 10 within 100 years (MLE):  (1.0699e-05, 0.9898) #> Growth rate (MLE):                                   (-0.038814, 0.043525) #> Environmental variance (MLE):                        (0.0070464, 0.020885) #> Unbiased variance:                                                       - #> AIC for the distribution of N:                                           - #>  #> --- Data Summary --- #>                               Value #> Current population size, nq:     47 #> xd = ln(nq / ne):            1.5476 #> Sample size, q + 1:              29 #>  #> --- Input Parameters --- #>                                                         Parameter #> Time unit:                                                  years #> Extinction threshold of population size, ne:                   10 #> Time window for extinction risk evaluation (years), th:     100.0 #> Significance level, alpha:                                   0.05 ext_di(dat, th = 100, ne = 10, qq_plot = TRUE) #> --- Estimates --- #>                                                       Estimate #> Probability of decline to 10 within 100 years (MLE):  0.096852 #> Growth rate (MLE):                                   0.0023556 #> Environmental variance (MLE):                          0.01087 #> Unbiased variance:                                    0.011273 #> AIC for the distribution of N:                          165.06 #>                                                                         CI #> Probability of decline to 10 within 100 years (MLE):  (1.0699e-05, 0.9898) #> Growth rate (MLE):                                   (-0.038814, 0.043525) #> Environmental variance (MLE):                        (0.0070464, 0.020885) #> Unbiased variance:                                                       - #> AIC for the distribution of N:                                           - #>  #> --- Data Summary --- #>                               Value #> Current population size, nq:     47 #> xd = ln(nq / ne):            1.5476 #> Sample size, q + 1:              29 #>  #> --- Input Parameters --- #>                                                         Parameter #> Time unit:                                                  years #> Extinction threshold of population size, ne:                   10 #> Time window for extinction risk evaluation (years), th:     100.0 #> Significance level, alpha:                                   0.05 ext_di(dat, th = 100, method = "oear") #> --- Estimates --- #>                                                                Estimate #> Probability of decline to 1 within 100 years (OEAR plug-in): 5.1099e-10 #> Growth rate (MLE):                                            0.0023556 #> Process variance (OEAR):                                      0.0043077 #> AR(1) pre-whitening rho:                                       -0.52522 #> Bartlett lag truncation (j):                                          4 #>                                                                                  CI #> Probability of decline to 1 within 100 years (OEAR plug-in): (1.6408e-23, 0.027088) #> Growth rate (MLE):                                            (-0.038814, 0.043525) #> Process variance (OEAR):                                     (0.0027924, 0.0082765) #> AR(1) pre-whitening rho:                                                          - what: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
title: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
text: Estimates demographic parameters extinction probability density-independent (drifted Wiener) model. time series population sizes, estimates growth rate variance using either naive MLE variance estimator observation-error--autocovariance-robust (OEAR) estimator, evaluates extinction risk horizon \(t^{\ast}\) confidence intervals \(w\)–\(z\) method. observation-error-free setting, naive \(w\)–\(z\) interval achieves near-nominal coverage, OEAR can robust additive observation error unknown short-run autocovariance.

title: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
code: ext_di(   dat,   ne = 1,   th = 100,   alpha = 0.05,   unit = "years",   qq_plot = FALSE,   formatted = TRUE,   digits = getOption("extr.digits", 5L),   method = c("naive", "oear") )

title: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
text: dat Data frame two numeric columns: time (strictly increasing) population size. Column names restricted. ne Numeric. Extinction threshold \(n_e \ge 1\). Default 1. th Numeric. Time horizon \(t^{\ast} > 0\). Default 100. alpha Numeric. Significance level \(\alpha \(0,1)\). Default 0.05. unit Character. Unit time (e.g., "years", "days", "generations"). Default "years". qq_plot Logical. TRUE, draws QQ-plot standardized increments check model assumptions (naive method ). Default FALSE. formatted Logical. TRUE, returns "ext_di" object; otherwise returns raw list. Default TRUE. digits Integer. Preferred significant digits printing. Affects display . Default getOption("extr.digits", 5). method Character. Variance estimation method. "naive" uses MLE variance. "oear" uses OEAR HAC long-run-variance estimator (AR(1) pre-whitening + Bartlett kernel). Default "naive".

title: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
text: list (class "ext_di" formatted=TRUE) containing extinction-risk estimates diagnostics. Core elements include Growth.rate, Variance, Unbiased.variance, lower_cl_s, upper_cl_s, linear_g, log_g, log_q, ci_linear_g, ci_log_g, ci_log_q, data/input summaries (nq, xd, sample.size, unit, ne, th, alpha). Variance method-dependent (naive MLE method="naive", OEAR HAC estimate method="oear"). aic reported method="naive" NA method="oear". method_diag NULL naive contains rho_tilde_pw j oear. lower_cl_s upper_cl_s chi-square based: exact method="naive" model assumptions, pragmatic plug-approximations method="oear".

title: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
text: Population dynamics follow $$dX=\mu\,dt+\sigma\,dW,$$ \(X(t)=\log N(t)\) \(W\) Wiener process. Extinction risk $$G=\Pr[T\le t^{\ast}\mid N(0)=n_0,n_e,\mu,\sigma],$$ probability population size falls \(n_e\) within \(t^{\ast}\). Irregular observation intervals allowed. function first estimates \(\mu\), estimates variance quantity according method: naive: uses drifted-Wiener MLE variance (Dennis et al., 1991). oear: uses observation-error--autocovariance-robust (OEAR) HAC long-run-variance estimator AR(1) pre-whitening Bartlett kernel (Hakoyama, press). method = "oear", robustness additive observation error relies long-run-variance cancellation differenced observation error (McNamara Harding, 2004), robustness unknown short-run autocovariance comes HAC estimation long-run variance. Extinction probability computed \(G(w,z)\) (Lande Orzack, 1988), confidence intervals constructed using \(w\)–\(z\) method (Hakoyama, press).

title: Extinction Risk Estimation for a Density-Independent Model — ext_di
text: Andrews, D. W. K. (1991). Heteroskedasticity autocorrelation consistent covariance matrix estimation. Econometrica, 59(3), 817-858. Dennis, B., Munholland, P.L., Scott, J.M. (1991) Estimation growth extinction parameters endangered species. Ecological Monographs, 61, 115-143. Hakoyama, H. Confidence intervals extinction risk: validating population viability analysis limited data. Methods Ecology Evolution. press. Preprint, doi:10.48550/arXiv.2509.09965 Lande, R. Orzack, S.H. (1988) Extinction dynamics age-structured populations fluctuating environment. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 85(19), 7418–7421. McNamara, J. M. Harding, K. C. (2004). Measurement error estimates population extinction risk. Ecology Letters, 7(1), 16-20. Newey, W. K. West, K. D. (1987). simple, positive semi-definite, heteroskedasticity autocorrelation consistent covariance matrix. Econometrica, 55(3), 703-708.

what: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
title: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
text: Compute \(G(w,z)\) complement \(Q(w,z)=1-G(w,z)\) density-independent (drifted Wiener) model, linear log scales.

title: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
code: ext_prob_di(w, z)  log_ext_prob_di(w, z)  log_ext_comp_di(w, z)  ext_prob_format_di(w, z, digits = 5L)

title: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
text: w Numeric; transformed parameter \(w=(\mu t+x_d)/(\sigma\sqrt{t})\). z Numeric; transformed parameter \(z=(-\mu t+x_d)/(\sigma\sqrt{t})\). digits Integer; significant digits formatting (ext_prob_format_di).

title: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
text: ext_prob_di: numeric scalar \(G(w,z)\). log_ext_prob_di: numeric scalar \(\log G(w,z)\). log_ext_comp_di: numeric scalar \(\log Q(w,z)\). ext_prob_format_di: character string formatted display.

title: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
text: \(t>0\) \(w+z>0\), $$   \Pr[T \leq t] = G(w,z)=\Phi(-w)+   \exp\!\left(\tfrac{z^2-w^2}{2}\right)\Phi(-z), \qquad   Q(w,z)=1-G(w,z). $$ \(\Phi\) \(\phi\) denote standard normal CDF PDF. Stability strategy. () large \(z\), rewrite product \(\exp((z^2-w^2)/2)\,\Phi(-z)\) via Mills ratio replace 8-term asymptotic series \(z \ge 19\). (ii) log scale, use log-sum-exp stable log-difference (log1mexp) built log1p/expm1 retain tail info. Domain. Scalar inputs assumed require \(w+z>0\). Functions. ext_prob_di(w,z): returns \(G(w,z)\) (linear scale). log_ext_prob_di(w,z): returns \(\log G(w,z)\). log_ext_comp_di(w,z): returns \(\log Q(w,z)\). ext_prob_format_di(w,z,digits): formats point estimate using repr_mode() format_by_mode().

title: Computes Extinction Probability for a Density-Independent Model — extinction_probability_di
text: Hiroshi Hakoyama, hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com

title: extr: Extinction Risk Estimation — extr-package
text: Estimates extinction risk population time series drifted Wiener process using MLE observation-error--autocovariance-robust estimators, confidence intervals based w-z method.

title: extr: Extinction Risk Estimation — extr-package
text: Maintainer: Hiroshi Hakoyama hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Format a Probability using a Display Mode — format_by_mode
title: Format a Probability using a Display Mode — format_by_mode
text: Format probability (point estimate CI bound) according chosen representation mode. Allowed modes "linear_G", "log_G", "log_Q", "undefined". function performs inference; formats numeric values already computed (linear log). Notes arguments: log_g, log_q, ci_log_g, ci_log_q natural logarithms (log() R). "log_Q": upper bound \(G\) uses ci_log_q[1] lower bound uses ci_log_q[2]. ordering reflects \(Q = 1-G\) decreases \(G\) increases.

title: Format a Probability using a Display Mode — format_by_mode
code: format_by_mode(   mode,   kind,   linear_g,   log_g,   log_q,   ci_linear_g,   ci_log_g,   ci_log_q,   digits )

title: Format a Probability using a Display Mode — format_by_mode
text: mode Character scalar. One "linear_G", "log_G", "log_Q", "undefined". kind Character scalar. One "point", "lower", "upper". linear_g Numeric scalar. Point estimate linear scale. log_g Numeric scalar. log(G) (natural log). May non-finite. log_q Numeric scalar. log(Q) \(Q=1-G\) (natural log). May non-finite. ci_linear_g Numeric length-2: c(lower, upper) \(G\). ci_log_g Numeric length-2: c(lower_logG, upper_logG). ci_log_q Numeric length-2: c(upper_logQ, lower_logQ). digits Integer. Significant digits numeric formatting.

title: Format a Probability using a Display Mode — format_by_mode
text: character scalar formatted display. Returns "NA" required value non-finite missing.

title: Format a Probability using a Display Mode — format_by_mode
text: Hiroshi Hakoyama, hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com

title: OEAR Diffusion-Scale Estimator — oear_sigma2_hac
text: Compute OEAR (observation-error--autocovariance-robust) diffusion-scale estimate \(\tilde{\sigma}^2\) estimating long-run variance (LRV) standardized growth increments using AR(1) pre-whitening Bartlett (Newey-West) HAC estimator, followed recoloring.

title: OEAR Diffusion-Scale Estimator — oear_sigma2_hac
text: mu Numeric scalar. Estimated growth rate \(\hat{\mu}\) used center increments. delta_log_n Numeric vector length q. Log-scale increments \(\Delta Y_i\). tau Numeric vector length q. Sampling intervals \(\tau_i > 0\).

title: OEAR Diffusion-Scale Estimator — oear_sigma2_hac
text: list components: sigma2_tilde Numeric scalar. OEAR diffusion-scale estimate \(\tilde{\sigma}^2\). rho_tilde_pw Numeric scalar. Estimated AR(1) pre-whitening coefficient \(\tilde\rho_{\mathrm{pw}}\). j Integer. Selected Bartlett truncation lag $J$.

title: OEAR Diffusion-Scale Estimator — oear_sigma2_hac
text: estimator targets LRV standardized centered increment series $$U_i = (\Delta Y_i - \hat{\mu}\,\tau_i)/\sqrt{\tau_i}, \qquad =1,\ldots,q,$$ interpreted effective environmental variance per unit time. Implementation steps: Center \(u_i\) subtracting \(\bar u\) obtain \(\tilde u_i\). Estimate AR(1) pre-whitening coefficient \(\tilde\rho_{\mathrm{pw}}\) OLS \(\tilde u_i = \rho_{\mathrm{pw}}\,\tilde u_{-1} + \varepsilon_i\) form pre-whitened residuals \(\tilde\varepsilon_i\). Compute residual autocovariances Bartlett HAC LRV \(\widetilde{\mathcal C}^{(\varepsilon)}_{\mathrm{NW}}(J)\). Choose truncation lag \(J\) via Andrews (1991) AR(1) plug-rule specialized Bartlett window. Recolor original scale obtain $$\tilde{\sigma}^2           = \widetilde{\mathcal C}_{\mathrm{NW}}(J)           = \widetilde{\mathcal C}^{(\varepsilon)}_{\mathrm{NW}}(J)/             (1-\tilde\rho_{\mathrm{pw}})^2.$$ construction designed robust short-run autocovariance , additive observation error log scale, cancellation property LRV increments appropriately centered.

title: OEAR Diffusion-Scale Estimator — oear_sigma2_hac
text: Andrews, D. W. K. (1991). Heteroskedasticity autocorrelation consistent covariance matrix estimation. Econometrica, 59(3), 817–858. Newey, W. K. West, K. D. (1987). simple, positive semi-definite, heteroskedasticity autocorrelation consistent covariance matrix. Econometrica, 55(3), 703–708.

title: Print Extinction Risk Estimates — print.ext_di
text: S3 method prints formatted summary ext_di object. output includes plug-extinction probability CI, method-specific parameter estimates, brief data summary, input settings.

title: Print Extinction Risk Estimates — print.ext_di
code: # S3 method for class 'ext_di' print(x, digits = NULL, ...)

title: Print Extinction Risk Estimates — print.ext_di
text: x object class "ext_di" returned ext_di. digits Integer. Significant digits display; defaults x$digits NULL. ... Additional arguments (currently ignored).

title: Print Extinction Risk Estimates — print.ext_di
text: Invisibly returns x printing formatted results.

what: Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
title: Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
text: pair internal functions formatting extinction probabilities: repr_mode() decides represent probability \(G\) based magnitude, format_by_mode() formats numbers accordingly presentation. functions affect display alter underlying calculations.

title: Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
text: g Numeric scalar, probability unit interval. May non-finite.

title: Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
text: repr_mode() returns character scalar, one : "linear_G" – display \(G\) linear scale; "log_G" – display \(G\) log10 scale tiny probabilities; "log_Q" – display \(Q=1-G\) log10 scale \(G \approx 1\); "undefined" – input finite. format_by_mode() returns character scalar formatted according selected mode kind value (point estimate, lower, upper CI bound).

title: Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
text: Probabilities close 0 1 displayed log scale improve readability. thresholds fixed 1e-300 ("near zero") 1 - 1e-15 ("near one"). Non-finite inputs reported "undefined".

title: Display Modes for Extinction Probabilities — repr_mode
text: Hiroshi Hakoyama, hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com

what: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
title: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
text: Estimators \(w\) \(z\) statistics used extinction probability calculations density-independent population model.

title: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
code: w_statistic(mu, xd, s, th)  z_statistic(mu, xd, s, th)

title: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
text: mu numeric: Estimated population growth rate, \(\hat{\mu}\). xd numeric: Distance extinction threshold log scale, \(x_d = \log(n_q / n_e)\). s numeric: Estimated environmental variance, \(\hat{\sigma}^2\). th numeric: Time horizon extinction probability evaluation, denoted \(t^{\ast}\).

title: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
text: numeric: Value statistic.

title: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
text: statistics defined $$ \hat w = \frac{\hat \mu t^{\ast} + x_d}{\sqrt{\hat \sigma^2 t^{\ast}}}, \qquad \hat z = \frac{- \hat \mu t^{\ast} + x_d}{\sqrt{\hat \sigma^2 t^{\ast}}}. $$

title: Computes the w and z Statistics — statistics_di
text: Hiroshi Hakoyama, hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com

what: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
title: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
text: Computes confidence intervals (CIs) extinction probability density-independent (drifted Wiener) model using w–z method, provides formatter display-ready CI strings.

title: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
code: confidence_interval_wz_di(mu, xd, s, th, tq, qq, alpha, prob_fun = ext_prob_di)  ci_wz_format_di(mu, xd, s, th, tq, qq, alpha, digits = 5L)

title: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
text: mu Numeric. Estimated growth rate \(\hat{\mu}\). xd Numeric. Log-distance threshold \(x_d=\log(n_q/n_e)\). s Numeric. Estimated environmental variance \(\hat{\sigma}^2\). th Numeric. Time horizon \(t^{\ast}\) evaluation. tq Numeric. Observation span \(t_q\) (first last time). qq Integer. Number intervals \(q\) (sample size minus 1). alpha Numeric. Significance level \(\alpha\)\,=\,1\,-\,CL. prob_fun Function. One ext_prob_di, log_ext_prob_di, log_ext_comp_di. digits Integer. Significant digits formatting (used ci_wz_format_di).

title: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
text: confidence_interval_wz_di: numeric vector c(lower, upper) chosen scale (natural log log_*). ci_wz_format_di: named character vector c(lower, upper) values preformatted display.

title: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
text: w–z method derives CIs inverting noncentral-\(t\) distributions transformed statistics \(w\) \(z\), combining form bounds \(G(w,z)\). Exact confidence intervals \(w\) \(z\) obtained numerically solving noncentral-\(t\) quantile equations corresponding observed statistics. CI \(G(w,z)\) approximated $$   \bigl( G(\overline{w},\,\underline{z}),\;          G(\underline{w},\,\overline{z}) \bigr), $$ \(\overline{w}\), \(\underline{w}\), \(\overline{z}\), \(\underline{z}\) upper lower confidence limits \(w\) \(z\), respectively. Across full parameter space, approach achieves near-nominal coverage. $z$ large positive, $G$ depends $w$, exact CIs available. argument prob_fun selects probability evaluated: ext_prob_di, log_ext_prob_di: CIs \(G(w,z)\) \(\log G(w,z)\); returned (lower, upper). log_ext_comp_di: CIs \(\log Q(w,z)= \log (1-G(w,z))\); returned (lower, upper).

title: Confidence Intervals for Extinction Probability (w–z, DI model) — wz_ci_di
text: Hiroshi Hakoyama, hiroshi.hakoyama@gmail.com

title: extr 1.1.0 (2026-03-14)
text: Added method = "oear" ext_di(), allowing extinction probability estimates associated confidence intervals computed using observation-error--autocovariance-robust (OEAR) plug-variance estimate. Implemented OEAR variance estimator using HAC long-run variance estimation AR(1) pre-whitening Bartlett kernel. Added OEAR diagnostics (rho_tilde_pw j) returned object printed output. Updated method-specific output: method = "naive", output includes Variance aic; method = "oear", output includes Process variance (OEAR), reports aic NA, reports lower_cl_s upper_cl_s using chi-square-based pragmatic plug-approximation.

title: extr 1.0.0 (2025-09-15)
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-21

title: extr 1.0.0 (2025-09-15)
text: Core extinction-risk estimator ext_di density-independent (drifted Wiener) models. Includes MLEs growth rate environmental variance irregular time series. Includes point estimate extinction probability. Includes confidence intervals extinction probability (w-z method) near-nominal coverage. Includes optional QQ-plot diagnostic.

title: extr 1.0.0 (2025-09-15)
text: Adaptive probability representation G, log G, log(1-G). Uses complement form G near 0 1 maintain numerical accuracy. Log-scale representation extends safe range symbolic exp(-DBL_MAX). Probabilities near 0 1 displayed 10^-* 1-10^-*. Printed precision configurable via options("extr.digits").
